mapping.a_element: field-theoretic methods based on the Doi-Peliti representation of the master equation for stochastic particle interaction models
mapping.b_element: non-perturbative renormalization group (NPRG) formalism for stochastic models

mapping.a_element: mapping the action near the absorbing state phase transition to Reggeon field theory
mapping.b_element: NPRG framework applied to critical dynamics and non-equilibrium steady states

mapping.a_element: fluctuation-induced renormalizations of population oscillation frequency, damping, and diffusion coefficients
mapping.b_element: NPRG analysis of critical dynamics and renormalization of parameters in non-equilibrium systems

mapping.a_element: continuous non-equilibrium phase transition governed by directed-percolation universality class
mapping.b_element: critical dynamics in non-equilibrium steady states studied via NPRG

mapping.a_element: stochastic noise and spatial structure in predator-prey models
mapping.b_element: stochastic models in non-equilibrium steady states

brief_justification: Both papers employ field-theoretic and renormalization group methods to analyze stochastic, spatially structured non-equilibrium systems. Paper A uses the Doi-Peliti representation and maps to Reggeon field theory near phase transitions, while Paper B develops the NPRG framework for non-equilibrium steady states, with both addressing fluctuation-induced renormalizations and critical dynamics.
